message: chore(beads,ftui-tty): update in-progress status + simplify panic tests

files.patch:
@@ -1335,7 +1335,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-lff4p.10.4","title":"Implement: PTY bridge server (Rust) with websocket transport","description":"Goal
- Implement a Rust service that spawns a PTY and bridges I/O over websockets to FrankenTerm.WASM.

Scope
- Use portable-pty (already in tree) or a better alternative if justified.
- Enforce security constraints from the threat model.
- Expose structured telemetry: bytes in/out, latency, resize counts, exit status.

Acceptance Criteria
- Can run locally and drive interactive shells.
- Produces JSONL logs conforming to the shared schema.

 {"id":"bd-lff4p.10.5","title":"E2E: scripted remote terminal sessions + golden transcripts","description":"Goal
- Add end-to-end scripts that connect the browser FrankenTerm to the PTY bridge and run deterministic scripted sessions.

Requirements
- Scripts must emit detailed JSONL (run_id, git_sha, sizes, wire counters, checksums).
- Golden transcripts are checksum-gated; failures produce actionable diffs.

Acceptance Criteria
- At least 5 high-signal scripts: resize storm, paste, unicode, OSC8 links, and long scrollback.

 {"id":"bd-lff4p.10.6","title":"Perf: keystroke-to-photon latency budget + measurement harness","description":"Goal
- Define and enforce latency budgets for interactive remote sessions.

Metrics
- Keystroke -> rendered frame (p50/p95/p99).
- Round-trip websocket latency and jitter.
- Output throughput under load.

Acceptance Criteria
- Harness outputs machine-readable JSON and is gated against regressions.

-{"id":"bd-lff4p.10.7","title":"Spec: TERM/capability profile + terminfo for FrankenTerm remote sessions","description":"Goal
- Ensure remote PTY apps behave correctly by defining FrankenTerm's terminal identity and capabilities.

Scope
- Decide what TERM will be set to in the PTY (e.g. `frankenterm`, `xterm-256color`, or a compatibility alias).
- If `TERM=frankenterm`, define and ship a terminfo entry (and a versioning story).
- Map terminfo capabilities to the VT/ANSI support matrix and reply engine behavior.

Acceptance Criteria
- A pinned capability profile exists (colors, mouse, paste, focus, kitty, hyperlinks) with explicit "must" vs "best-effort" behaviors.
- E2E remote scripts validate that common CLI apps render correctly under the chosen TERM profile.

+{"id":"bd-lff4p.10.7","title":"Spec: TERM/capability profile + terminfo for FrankenTerm remote sessions","description":"Goal
- Ensure remote PTY apps behave correctly by defining FrankenTerm's terminal identity and capabilities.

Scope
- Decide what TERM will be set to in the PTY (e.g. `frankenterm`, `xterm-256color`, or a compatibility alias).
- If `TERM=frankenterm`, define and ship a terminfo entry (and a versioning story).
- Map terminfo capabilities to the VT/ANSI support matrix and reply engine behavior.

Acceptance Criteria
- A pinned capability profile exists (colors, mouse, paste, focus, kitty, hyperlinks) with explicit "must" vs "best-effort" behaviors.
- E2E remote scripts validate that common CLI apps render correctly under the chosen TERM profile.

 {"id":"bd-lff4p.10.8","title":"Alien Artifact: queueing-theoretic backpressure + fairness policy (remote protocol)","description":"Goal
- Design backpressure/flow-control for the websocket protocol so we never OOM and we maintain low interactive latency under load.

Techniques (Alien Artifact)
- Queueing model for input/output queues (arrival/service rates, tail latency).
- Explicit objective: minimize expected loss with an asymmetric loss matrix:
  - huge penalty for OOM/unbounded memory
  - large penalty for keystroke latency spikes
  - smaller penalty for throughput loss
- Use an evidence ledger: record measured rates, chosen thresholds, and why.

Deliverables
- A spec for bounded queues + drop/merge policy (e.g. coalesce resize/mouse-move events, chunk outputs).
- A fairness policy so long outputs don't starve input (interactive priority).

Acceptance Criteria
- Stress harness demonstrates bounded memory and stable p95 keystroke latency under worst-case output floods.
- Policy is explainable: given metrics, we can show why a drop/coalesce happened.
","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:24:42.047548858Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T09:24:42.047548858Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.10.8","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.10","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:24:42.047548858Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.10.8","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.10.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:24:42.047548858Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.10.8","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:24:42.047548858Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-lff4p.2","title":"FrankenTerm Web: WebGPU renderer + web input plumbing","description":"Scope
- Build `frankenterm-web`: a WebGPU-first terminal renderer + input stack compiled to WASM (no xterm.js).

User-Facing Requirements
- Crisp text + stable geometry under resize/zoom/DPR changes.
- Smooth scrollback (virtualized rendering; no O(n) over history per frame).
- Selection + copy/paste, OSC 8 hyperlinks (hover/click), and in-terminal search.
- Accessibility strategy despite canvas/WebGPU rendering (DOM mirror mode, focus, high-contrast, reduced motion).

Deliverables
- `frankenterm-web` crate exporting a minimal JS/WASM API: init, resize, input, render/present.
- WebGPU renderer (glyph atlas + instancing) with deterministic patch application.
- Input capture: keyboard (modifiers/repeat), mouse + wheel, touch; clipboard; IME/composition.
- Bench + E2E harnesses emitting detailed JSONL for correctness + perf gates.
","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:49:17.880523076Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T09:16:03.657124887Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:49:17.880523076Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-lff4p.2.1","title":"ADR: FrankenTerm WebGPU renderer architecture (glyph atlas, batching, present)","description":"Goal
- Decide and document the renderer design for frankenterm-web.

Must Cover
- Rendering primitive set: background rects, text glyph quads, underline/strikethrough, cursor, selection.
- Glyph pipeline: bitmap vs SDF/MSDF; atlas packing; eviction; metrics.
- Batching: instancing strategy, buffer update strategy (full vs dirty spans), CPU->GPU transfer budgets.
- Color management: sRGB, blending rules for selections/cursor.

Acceptance Criteria
- Renderer tasks can be implemented without ambiguity.
- Includes a measurable frame budget and how we will profile it.
","notes":"Drafted ADR-009 at docs/adr/ADR-009-webgpu-renderer-architecture.md (WebGPU-first, glyph atlas, instanced quads, patch-driven updates, determinism gates via patch/frame hashes).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"PinkMill","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:51:40.955523135Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T18:49:30.896274992Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T18:49:30.896256127Z","close_reason":"Completed: ADR-009 WebGPU renderer architecture","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.1","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:51:40.955523135Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.1","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:51:40.955523135Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.1","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:51:40.955523135Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-lff4p.4.3","title":"Implement: Unix input reader (poll + bytes) feeding ftui-core InputParser","description":"Goal
- Replace Crossterm event polling with a tight byte-reader + ftui-core parsing.

Requirements
- Non-blocking or poll-based read with timeout.
- Convert bytes to ftui-core Event via existing InputParser.
- Support resize, focus, bracketed paste, mouse sequences.

Acceptance Criteria
- Input parity tests vs current behavior for core keys and mouse sequences.
- No busy-looping when idle.
","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:54:27.260346524Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T19:27:42.783663257Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T19:27:42.484300471Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.4.3","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:54:27.260346524Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.4.3","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:54:27.260346524Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.4.3","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:54:27.260346524Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1006,"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.4.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"CLOSED by PinkElk. Commit 73ad29bf: poll(2) + InputParser integration, VecDeque event buffer, pipe-based from_reader test constructor, 10 input parity tests (ASCII, arrows, Ctrl, F-keys, SGR mouse, focus, paste, scroll, modifiers, buffered events). 24 total tests pass.","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:27:42Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-lff4p.4.4","title":"Implement: resize detection + size queries (SIGWINCH)","description":"Goal
- Provide accurate, low-latency resize handling without Crossterm.

Requirements
- Query terminal size via ioctl.
- Deliver resize events via SIGWINCH or equivalent.

Acceptance Criteria
- Stress test: resize storm does not drop or reorder events; no panics.
","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:54:32.571651561Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T19:33:09.679470486Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T19:33:09.395855322Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.4.4","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:54:32.571651561Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.4.4","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:54:32.571651561Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1007,"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.4.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"CLOSED by PinkElk (w/ co-agent). Commit f0aeaed2: SIGWINCH via signal-hook thread + mpsc, ioctl size via rustix::tcgetwinsize, time-sliced poll for resize latency, push_resize with dedup, 4 resize tests (storm coalescing stress included). 29 total tests pass.","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:33:09Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-lff4p.4.5","title":"Integrate: swap ftui runtime to new backend (native path)","description":"Goal
- Wire the new native backend into `ftui-runtime` and remove all direct Crossterm coupling from the runtime hot path.

Non-Negotiable Compatibility
- Behavior must match current demo semantics (input, resize, mouse, paste, focus, kitty keyboard, alt-screen/inline rules).
- One-writer rule and terminal cleanup semantics must remain correct under normal exit and panic/unwind.

Testing + Logging
- Run the existing PTY E2E suite (`tests/e2e/scripts/run_all.sh` or curated subsets) against the new backend.
- E2E logs must validate against `tests/e2e/lib/e2e_jsonl_schema.json` and include backend identity + capability profile.
- Add/extend tests as needed to cover any behavior differences discovered during migration.

Acceptance Criteria
- `ftui-demo-showcase` runs on the new backend with identical user-visible behavior.
- E2E suite is green and produces detailed JSONL logs usable for regression triage.

- Prove lifecycle correctness under real PTY conditions.

- Tests validate cleanup on panic/unwind paths.

 {"id":"bd-lff4p.4.8","title":"Optimize: native IO (syscall minimization, buffering, writev)","description":"Goal
- After Crossterm removal, push native IO performance beyond prior baselines.

Acceptance Criteria
- Measured present-time reductions (p95) in demo showcase.
